test_scripts.test_case_title: Verify User Registration with Valid Data
test_scripts.generation_notes: Test case requires a generic user registration form with username, email, password, confirm_password, first_name, last_name, phone_number, and country fields. The AURA framework does NOT have a RegistrationPage page object - it uses HPIDPage for HP ID authentication which only has email, password, firstName, and lastName fields. Created new RegistrationPage and SuccessPage page objects with all required locators and methods. The test script uses direct locator access via BasePageObject.__getattr__ pattern and Playwright expect() assertions with explicit timeouts. All 10 test steps are implemented exactly as specified in the test case analysis.

new_page_objects.reason: KB confirmed no existing page object has the required fields for this test case. HPIDPage only has email, password, firstName, lastName but lacks username, confirm_password, phone_number, and country fields. Created complete RegistrationPage with all 10 required locators and 11 methods.

KB confirmed no generic SuccessPage exists for registration confirmation. ThankYouPage exists but is specific to enrollment flow. Created SuccessPage with success_message locator and verification methods for step 10.

integration_instructions: a. Create folder test_flows/TestTCREG001UserRegistration/ in AURA-FRAMEWORK
a. Copy additional_generations/new_pages/registration_page.py to pages/ in AURA-FRAMEWORK
b. Copy additional_generations/new_pages/success_page.py to pages/ in AURA-FRAMEWORK
4. Update the registration page URL in registration_page.py navigate_to_registration_page() method to match your application's actual registration URL
5. Verify locator selectors in both page objects match your application's actual HTML structure
6. Run the test to verify all locators are correctly identified

7. IMPORTANT: This test case requires a generic registration form. If your application uses HP ID authentication (HPIDPage), you may need to adapt the test case to remove username, confirm_password, phone_number, and country fields and use HPIDHelper.sign_up() instead
critical_notes: MAPPING REPORT NOT AVAILABLE: The final_graphify_report.json file was not found in the repository. Generation proceeded using only test_case_analysis.json and file_resolution_report.json.

FRAMEWORK MISMATCH: The AURA framework is designed for HP Instant Ink enrollment flows using HP ID authentication. This test case requires a generic user registration form with fields that do not exist in the HP ID flow.

LOCATOR VERIFICATION REQUIRED: All locators in RegistrationPage and SuccessPage use fallback selector chains (data-testid > id > CSS). These MUST be verified against the actual application HTML structure before running the test.
URL CONFIGURATION REQUIRED: The navigate_to_registration_page() method uses a placeholder '/register' URL. This must be updated to match your application's actual registration page URL.
ALTERNATIVE APPROACH: If your application uses HP ID authentication, consider adapting the test case to use existing HPIDPage and HPIDHelper instead of creating new page objects.
